title: 2-Hydroxy-4-Methylbenzophenoneoxime as Analytical Reagent : Gravimetric Determination of Copper
author: (Miss) L. C. JHAVERI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, M R. Science Institute, Gujarat College, Ahmedabad-380 006 
  

  Manuscript received 13 April 1983, accepted 3 December 1983  

 2-Hydroxy-4-methylbenzophenone oxime (HMBOX) has been used as a reagent for gravimetric estimation of copper and for its separation from other ions. Copper is quantitatively precipitated by HMBOX at  p H 3.0-8.0. After drying the precipitate at 110-120º, its composition corresponds to the formula Cu(C 14 H 12 O 2 N) 2 . Interference due to Fe(III) has been eliminated by masking with potassium tartrate and in other cases by suitable control of  p H. Copper and nickel were estimated in different alloys.
